skill.name: c4-container
skill.description: Creates C4 container-level architecture documentation from component information, deployment details, APIs, dependencies, and infrastructure context.
skill.summary: Document C4 container architecture, APIs, dependencies, and deployment boundaries.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, network, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples and architecture terminology in SKILL.md. No executable code, command invocation, secret handling, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis marked inline and fenced Markdown examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced lines contain documentation text, Mermaid diagram fences, endpoint examples, and an OpenAPI template, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance only, and the flagged backticks delimit documentation examples. I found no shell command construction or runtime execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Static analysis marked hardcoded URLs as network behavior. The URLs are a C4 Model reference link and an example OpenAPI server value, with no instruction to fetch data or transmit information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside documentation content and a template. There is no code or instruction that performs a network request.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography across description and API documentation lines. The evidence appears to be keyword matches inside ordinary words, placeholders, HTTP method examples, API descriptions, and architecture terminology.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content contains no cryptographic functions, algorithms, or credential handling. The matches are documentation words and example architecture labels.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system reconnaissance, but the lines describe C4 system diagrams and documentation output. They do not instruct the model to inspect host state, enumerate files, or gather environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term system is used in the C4 architecture sense. I found no reconnaissance commands or host discovery instructions.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose deployment context when component notes are scattered across files. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce clear C4 container documentation, diagrams, API summaries, and infrastructure notes.
actual_capabilities: Synthesizes component documentation into C4 container-level views.
Maps components to deployment units such as applications, databases, and queues.
Describes container interfaces, protocols, and API responsibilities.
Creates Mermaid C4Container diagram guidance for system deployment views.
Prompts for OpenAPI or Swagger-style API documentation where appropriate.
Captures dependencies, scaling notes, resources, and deployment configuration links.
limitations: It does not inspect infrastructure automatically without provided project files or context.
It does not validate Mermaid, OpenAPI, Docker, or Kubernetes syntax by itself.
It depends on accurate component-level documentation and deployment details.
It focuses on C4 container views, not full code-level architecture analysis.

use_cases.title: Create Deployment Views
use_cases.description: Turn component notes and deployment information into a container-level architecture document.

use_cases.title: Standardize Service Documentation
use_cases.description: Document microservice boundaries, protocols, APIs, and dependent systems in a consistent format.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Reviews
use_cases.description: Generate container diagrams and infrastructure summaries for review meetings and onboarding.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Container Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create C4 container-level documentation for this system. Use the component list, deployment notes, dependencies, and API details I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Map Components to Containers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these component descriptions and group them into deployment containers. Explain each boundary and identify the container type, technology, and responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Document Interfaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the container interface section for these services. Include protocols, API responsibilities, external systems, and links to available specifications.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Review Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a complete C4 container review package with container descriptions, dependency analysis, diagram guidance, deployment assumptions, risks, and missing information.

output_examples.input: A web application, an API service, a database, and a queue support an order management product.
output_examples.output: A container-level architecture document that names each deployment unit, explains responsibilities, lists protocols, and describes the main data flow.

output_examples.input: Several components have unclear deployment boundaries and multiple API integrations.
output_examples.output: A boundary recommendation that groups components into containers, identifies API ownership, and highlights missing deployment or interface details.

output_examples.input: A team needs an architecture review summary for a microservice system.
output_examples.output: A review-ready summary with container roles, dependencies, scaling considerations, diagram guidance, and questions for unresolved infrastructure choices.

best_practices: Provide component descriptions, deployment targets, technologies, and known dependencies before requesting synthesis.
Review generated container boundaries with engineers who own the deployment topology.
Keep diagrams and API documentation updated when services, protocols, or infrastructure change.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to invent deployment details that are not supported by project evidence.
Do not mix C4 container documentation with low-level class or function documentation.
Do not publish generated API details without validating them against implementation or specifications.

faq.question: What C4 level does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets the C4 Container level, which describes deployable units, responsibilities, technologies, and communication paths.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams?
faq.answer: It provides Mermaid C4Container diagram guidance and structure based on the system context you provide.

faq.question: Does it generate OpenAPI specifications?
faq.answer: It can help draft API documentation sections, but final specifications should be validated against the implemented API.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Component documentation, deployment manifests, service names, technology choices, protocols, dependencies, and known external systems work best.

faq.question: Is this only for microservices?
faq.answer: No. It can document monoliths, databases, queues, web applications, APIs, and other deployable container types.

faq.question: Can it replace architecture review?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare documentation, but human reviewers should confirm boundaries, risks, and deployment accuracy.
